excerpt: Both the sun and the moon move in their appointed orbits. God has ordained the lunar circle around the solar path—a relationship neither diminishes nor transgresses.

The fractional surplus—those accumulated half-days—possesses singular significance. Across the twelve months, these 1.5-day fractions accumulate to eighteen days (1.5 × 12 = 18), a measure equivalent to the holiest days of the year.
source_work: Tayyibi astronomical and spiritual science (internal KASHKOLE tradition)

excerpt: The Imam spoke words of ineffable tenderness: 'Your mother erred not in naming you Hurr—the Free One. You are free in this world, and free in the hereafter!'
source_authority: Shiite hadith literature and spiritual narratives
source_location_hint: Traditional accounts of Hurr ibn Yazid al-Riyahi

excerpt: When those gathering the fallen came upon his body, witnesses beheld a luminous radiance emanating from his face—his visage no longer dark but radiant. The odor that once was foul had become perfumed, as though the earth received the sweetness of his sacrifice.
The Prophet himself declared: 'Salman is of the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household)—Salman has become one of us.'
source_location_hint: Traditional accounts of Salman al-Farsi's relationship to the Ahl al-Bayt
